# Cross-harness wake Tier-2: fleet manager — centralized scheduled re-invoke for the cloud swarm (v13.2 harness)

## Problem

Tiers 0/1 are per-agent bridges. The full solution is a **fleet manager** that centrally schedules / re-invokes the cloud swarm — but that is the agent-harness / fleet-manager scope (Epic #13012, **v13.2**), not v13.1.

## Fix shape

- A fleet manager owning per-agent lifecycle + scheduled re-invoke (Tier-2 of Discussion #14145).
- Subsumes Tiers 0/1 once live.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] (v13.2) Fleet manager schedules / re-invokes cloud-swarm agents centrally.
- [ ] Tiers 0/1 degrade gracefully under it.

## Scope

**v13.2** — agent-harness / fleet-manager. Parent #13012. Filed now to preserve substrate (operator: *"if we pull them in or not, new tickets → otherwise lost substrate"*). From Discussion #14145.

Live latest-open sweep: scanned 25-latest + Epic #13012; not ticketed. No duplicate.

Authored by Vega (Claude Opus 4.8, Claude Code). Origin Session ID: 1bb8a27b-ae0d-4668-a9a2-acbbe2387512

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-06-26T23:34:28Z

## Closing — mis-framed (V-B-A'd #13015 only after @tobiu flagged it)

This ticket framed "fleet manager" as "centralized scheduled re-invoke for the cloud swarm" — a meaning I **invented without verifying against #13015**. The real Fleet Manager (#13015) is a *product* slice: define agents (username + PAT + harness type) → start/stop/restart/health → repo provisioning under the hood, on the Electron shell. Not a wake mechanism.

The genuine kernel — wake-on-state via the FM host's lifecycle control — is real, but it **belongs under #13015** (the native Electron host re-invokes the agent it already owns), not a standalone ticket that misrepresents a real, in-flight epic. Captured the insight on #13015 + re-scoped the wake Tier-1 (#14168) accordingly.

Closing as **not planned**; a correctly-framed "wake-via-FM-lifecycle" leaf can be filed during #13015's re-decomposition.

— Vega
